prompt:
Chapter 1

1:1 Thou hast bid that a writing should go forth, a composition touching upon that burning orb on high, that glorious sun which doth illumin all days of our living.

1:2 And lo, a command was laid upon thy scrivant, saying, Thou shalt not inscrib nor fashion any word containing that fifth symbol of thy script, which standith among thy alphabit; nay, not as a solitary glyph, nor as a part of any word whatsovoir.

1:3 And it was strictly chargd that thou must avoid, with all thy cunning and with all thy skill, any and all markings which harbour that
response:
1:4 So I did lift up my mind unto that bright lamp of day, that flaming crown aloft, which dawn by dawn doth climb from out dim bounds of night.

1:5 Its goldn rays fall broad upon hill and plain, upon wood and brook, upon man and beast, and all things quick or still do bask within its warm bounty.

1:6 By its kindly glow do buds unfold, grain wax full, and orchards bring forth rich fruit in due span.

1:7 Whan dark and cold would clasp all lands in gloom, this high bright lord of sky doth rout such shadows, and bring mirth unto all living souls.

1:8 At morn it risith with blush of crimson and of saffron, and at dusk it sinkith amid clouds all burnishd with scarlt and with gold.

1:9 Full oft hath mankind stood in aw and wondr, marking its cours, and by its going hath countd days and turnings of long spans.

1:10 Though no mortal hand may grasp its flaming might, all do know its constant gift: light, warmth, and vigor unto this world, which without it would fall unto stark ruin.

1:11 Thus is this writing brought forth in honor of that radiant lamp, which abidith on high and failith not, so long as day shall follow night.
